form-catalog.Description: Record of emergency contacts, beneficiaries for death gratuity, disposition of remains instructions, and insurance elections. Required for all active duty and reserve members.
form-catalog.Privacy Act Statement: 10 USC 1475-1480, 1489, 37 USC 401-408. Used to provide information to next of kin and beneficiaries upon casualty.

form-catalog.Form Title: Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty
form-catalog.Description: Certificate documenting military service history, discharge type, awards, and service dates. Issued at separation from active duty.
form-catalog.Privacy Act Statement: 10 USC 1169, EO 9397. Used to verify military service.

form-catalog.Form Title: Application for Department of Defense Common Access Card
form-catalog.Description: Application for issuance of DoD Common Access Card (CAC). Establishes identity and eligibility for DoD IT systems and physical access.
Self-reported medical history form used during accession, periodic health assessments, and separation physicals.
Claim form for reimbursement of official travel expenses including per diem, transportation, and incidental costs.
Structured risk assessment tool for mission planning. Identifies hazards, evaluates risk levels, and documents mitigation controls.
Request for access to DoD information systems. Documents need-to-know, security clearance, training completion, and commander approval.
Specifies security classification requirements for classified contracts. Defines access levels, storage requirements, and handling instructions.

disposition-instructions.Specific Instructions: Prefer burial with full military honors at Arlington National Cemetery if eligible. If not eligible, Nashville National Cemetery.
Cremation with ashes scattered at sea per Navy tradition. No religious ceremony requested.
Burial at Miramar National Cemetery, San Diego. Catholic ceremony requested.

dd-214.Decorations Medals Badges: Army Commendation Medal (2), Army Achievement Medal (3), Iraq Campaign Medal with 2 Campaign Stars, Global War on Terrorism Service Medal, National Defense Service Medal, Army Service Ribbon, Combat Action Badge
dd-214.Military Education: Warrior Leader Course, Basic Combat Engineer Course, Advanced Leader Course
dd-214.Authority and Reason: AR 635-200, Ch 4 - Completion of Required Active Service
dd-214.Remarks: Member transitioned to Army Reserve component. Awarded ARCOM for meritorious service during OIF deployment.

dd-214.Decorations Medals Badges: Army Achievement Medal, National Defense Service Medal, Army Service Ribbon, Expert Infantryman Badge, Air Assault Badge
dd-214.Military Education: Basic Leader Course, Air Assault School, Ranger School (Phase 1 complete)
dd-214.Remarks: Member reenlisted for 5-year term effective 2022-10-01. Previous term DD-214 issued per regulation.

dd-214.Decorations Medals Badges: Navy and Marine Corps Achievement Medal, Good Conduct Medal, National Defense Service Medal, Sea Service Deployment Ribbon (2), Navy Expert Pistol Medal
dd-214.Military Education: IT A School, Cyber Security Fundamentals, Navy Leadership Development Program
dd-214.Remarks: Member reenlisted for 4-year term. Promoted to PO2 during previous enlistment. Security clearance: Secret (current).

dd-2807-1.Hospitalization History: 2021-03 Right ankle fracture during training exercise, treated at Blanchfield ACH
dd-2807-1.Additional Explanations: Back pain intermittent since 2021 ankle injury, exacerbated by rucking. Manages with stretching and OTC medication. No duty limitations.
dd-2807-1.Examiner Summary: Healthy 31yo active duty male. Hx of right ankle fracture (healed), intermittent low back pain. All vitals WNL. No acute findings.
Healthy 22yo male, no significant medical history. Meets all accession standards for USMC enlistment.

dd-2807-1.Family Medical History: Mother: Breast cancer (survivor). Father: Coronary artery disease.
dd-2807-1.Additional Explanations: Headaches 2-3x/month, tension-type, managed with OTC. Mild hearing loss left ear from flight line exposure, documented in audiogram. Back pain related to prolonged sitting at analyst workstation. Anxiety: mild, situational, no medication.
dd-2807-1.Examiner Summary: 34yo female, separation physical. Mild high-frequency hearing loss L ear (flight line). Tension headaches and mild anxiety. All within normal limits for VA disability evaluation referral.
dd-2807-1.Examiner Recommendation: Fit for Separation - VA Referral Recommended

dd-2807-1.Examiner Summary: Healthy 35yo female officer. No medical issues identified. All screening labs within normal limits.

dd-1351-2.Itinerary Summary: Fayetteville Regional Airport to San Antonio International. AMEDDC&S Trauma Medicine Symposium, Brooke Army Medical Center.
dd-1351-2.Remarks: Conference attendance approved per CG memorandum dtd 2025-12-15.

dd-1351-2.Itinerary Summary: POV from Fort Campbell to Fort Moore. Senior Leader Course preparation seminar, 3 days.
dd-1351-2.Remarks: Government lodging utilized on-post (no cost). POV authorized due to equipment transport requirement.

dd-1351-2.Itinerary Summary: Boise Airport to Reagan National. DIA Annual Intelligence Professionals Symposium, DIA HQ Bolling AFB.
dd-1351-2.Remarks: Metro used for local transportation. Baggage fee for classified materials container.

dd-2977.Mission Task Description: Company-level live fire exercise with combined arms integration at Range 44, Fort Campbell. Includes M4 qualification, M240B familiarization, and squad tactical movement under fire.
dd-2977.Hazard 1 Description: Live ammunition and pyrotechnics on range
dd-2977.Hazard 1 Controls: Range safety officer present at all times. Mandatory safety brief. Controlled ammunition distribution. Medical standby with MEDEVAC on call.
dd-2977.Hazard 2 Description: Heat injury due to high ambient temperature and physical exertion
dd-2977.Hazard 2 Controls: Wet bulb globe temperature monitoring every 30 min. Mandatory hydration schedule. Work/rest cycle enforcement. Aid station with IV capability.
dd-2977.Hazard 3 Description: Vehicle movement in training area with dismounted personnel
dd-2977.Hazard 3 Controls: Ground guides for all vehicle movement. Designated pedestrian routes. Vehicle speed limit 10mph in assembly area. Road guards at crossing points.
dd-2977.Hazard 4 Description: Noise-induced hearing damage from weapons fire
dd-2977.Hazard 4 Controls: Double hearing protection mandatory (foam plugs + over-ear). Hearing protection compliance checks prior to firing. Post-exercise hearing screening.
dd-2977.Supervision Plan: OIC and RSO maintain continuous oversight. NCOs conduct checks at each phase transition. Safety stand-down authority at platoon level and above.
dd-2977.Feedback and Lessons Learned: Range executed safely. One heat casualty treated and returned to duty. Recommend earlier start time for summer iterations.
dd-2977.Remarks: After action review scheduled for D+3.

dd-2977.Mission Task Description: Platoon-level amphibious beach landing exercise at Camp Pendleton Red Beach. Includes AAV waterborne operations, beach assault, and casualty evacuation drill.
dd-2977.Hazard 1 Description: Drowning risk during waterborne AAV operations
dd-2977.Hazard 1 Controls: All Marines wear flotation devices. Safety swimmers in water. Rescue craft on standby. AAV crew certified and current. Pre-operation swim qualification verified.
dd-2977.Hazard 2 Description: Crush/entrapment inside AAV during surf zone transit
dd-2977.Hazard 2 Controls: Emergency egress drill conducted prior to operation. Cargo hatch unsealed during transit. AAV crew chief monitors all passengers. Maximum capacity enforced.
dd-2977.Hazard 3 Description: Soft tissue injuries during beach assault movement
dd-2977.Hazard 3 Controls: Terrain reconnaissance conducted. Known obstacles marked. Corpsman with each squad. CASEVAC plan briefed to all hands.
dd-2977.Supervision Plan: Company commander as overall exercise OIC. Each AAV has designated vehicle commander. Shore party includes safety officer with abort authority.
dd-2977.Remarks: Weather window required: sea state 3 or below. Go/no-go decision at H-2.

dd-2977.Mission Task Description: Reserve unit annual training: bridge construction exercise over Kankakee River. 3-day field exercise with heavy equipment operation and waterway crossing.
dd-2977.Hazard 1 Description: Heavy equipment operation (crane, bulldozer) near waterway
dd-2977.Hazard 1 Controls: Only licensed operators. Ground guides required. 15ft setback from bank edge. Equipment inspection prior to operation.
dd-2977.Hazard 2 Description: Drowning/cold water immersion during bridge emplacement
dd-2977.Hazard 2 Controls: PFDs mandatory within 10ft of water. Safety boat on standby. Buddy system enforced. Water temperature check - cold weather protocol if below 60F.
dd-2977.Hazard 3 Description: Struck-by hazard from bridge components during assembly
dd-2977.Hazard 3 Controls: Hard hats and steel-toe boots mandatory. Rigging inspection by certified rigger. Exclusion zone during crane lifts. Hand signals standardized per TM.
dd-2977.Supervision Plan: Project OIC on-site at all times. Each work section has NCOIC. Safety officer conducts hourly spot checks. Stand-down authority at section leader level.
dd-2977.Feedback and Lessons Learned: Exercise completed safely. Recommend additional ground guide training for reserve operators with less than annual equipment hours.
dd-2977.Remarks: Coordination with USACE and local authorities completed.

dd-2875.System Description: Navy Marine Corps Intranet - standard desktop environment and email services
dd-2875.Justification: IT rating requires privileged access to manage NMCI workstations, troubleshoot network issues, and administer local accounts for CVN-70 shipboard users.

dd-2875.System Description: Joint Worldwide Intelligence Communications System - TS/SCI network for intelligence analysis and dissemination
dd-2875.Justification: 1N0X1 All Source Intelligence Analyst requires JWICS access for daily intelligence production, threat analysis, and classified reporting in support of 366th FW mission.

dd-2875.System Description: Army Training Requirements and Resources System - training management and course enrollment
dd-2875.Justification: NCO requires ATRRS access to manage platoon training records, enroll soldiers in required courses, and track readiness metrics for the company training program.

dd-2875.System Description: Military Health System electronic health record - patient care documentation and medical order entry
dd-2875.Justification: Physician requires MHS GENESIS provider-level access for patient care documentation, medication ordering, lab result review, and clinical decision support at Womack AMC.
